aids.title: Cache Mapping Types
aids.content:
**D**irect = **D**efinite location (one choice)
**F**ully = **F**ree to go anywhere
**S**et = **S**everal choices (n ways)

"D-F-S: Definite-Free-Several"

aids.title: 3 Cs of Cache Misses
aids.content:
**C**ompulsory = **C**old start (first access)
**C**apacity = **C**an't fit all data
**C**onflict = **C**ollision in same set

"Cold, Can't fit, Collision"

aids.title: The Bookshelf Analogy
aids.content:
Cache mapping is like organizing books:

**Direct-mapped**: Each book has one assigned spot.
If spot is taken, old book must go!

**Fully associative**: Put books anywhere.
But finding a book takes time (search all).

**Set-associative**: Books go on assigned shelf.
But anywhere on that shelf is OK.

aids.title: Quick Address Bit Calculation
aids.content:
**Offset bits** = log2(line size)
**Index bits** = log2(number of sets)
**Tag bits** = Address bits - Offset - Index

Example: 32-bit, 64B line, 256 sets
- Offset = log2(64) = 6
- Index = log2(256) = 8
- Tag = 32 - 6 - 8 = 18

aids.title: Write Policies Comparison
aids.content:
```
Write-Through:        Write-Back:
   CPU                   CPU
    |                     |
    v                     v
 [Cache]------------->[Cache] + Dirty bit
    |                     |
    v (every write)       v (on eviction)
 [Memory]             [Memory]
```
Through = immediate, Back = delayed

aids.title: Cache Miss Rhyme
aids.content:
First time access, that's **compulsory**,
Cache too small? That's **capacity**!
Two blocks fight for same position,
That's a **conflict** - bad condition!

Preetch for cold, grow for small,
More ways stop the conflict brawl!
